#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Test script to validate the cuDNN runtime version exposure in CUDA hooks.
This verifies that the PR changes are correctly implemented across all 7 files.

PR: [cuDNN][SDPA][Convolution] Expose cuDNN runtime version in CUDA hooks (#167327)
"""
import sys

def main():
    print("Testing cuDNN runtime version exposure in CUDA hooks...")
    print()

    errors = []

    # Check Context.h has versionRuntimeCuDNN
    with open('aten/src/ATen/Context.h', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('Context.h: missing versionRuntimeCuDNN()')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] Context.h has versionRuntimeCuDNN')
        if 'versionCuDNNFrontend()' not in content:
            errors.append('Context.h: missing versionCuDNNFrontend()')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] Context.h has versionCuDNNFrontend')

    # Check CUDAHooks.cpp has versionRuntimeCuDNN implementation
    with open('aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.cpp', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'CUDAHooks::versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooks.cpp: missing versionRuntimeCuDNN implementation')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooks.cpp has versionRuntimeCuDNN implementation')
        if 'cudnnGetVersion()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooks.cpp: missing cudnnGetVersion() call')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooks.cpp uses cudnnGetVersion()')
        if 'CUDAHooks::versionCuDNNFrontend()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooks.cpp: missing versionCuDNNFrontend implementation')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooks.cpp has versionCuDNNFrontend implementation')

    # Check CUDAHooks.h has versionRuntimeCuDNN declaration
    with open('aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.h', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooks.h: missing versionRuntimeCuDNN declaration')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooks.h has versionRuntimeCuDNN declaration')
        if 'versionCuDNNFrontend()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooks.h: missing versionCuDNNFrontend declaration')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooks.h has versionCuDNNFrontend declaration')

    # Check CUDAHooksInterface.h has virtual versionRuntimeCuDNN
    with open('aten/src/ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'virtual long versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooksInterface.h: missing virtual versionRuntimeCuDNN')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooksInterface.h has virtual versionRuntimeCuDNN')
        if 'virtual long versionCuDNNFrontend()' not in content:
            errors.append('CUDAHooksInterface.h: missing virtual versionCuDNNFrontend')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] CUDAHooksInterface.h has virtual versionCuDNNFrontend')

    # Check Convolution.cpp uses versionRuntimeCuDNN
    with open('aten/src/ATen/native/Convolution.cpp', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('Convolution.cpp: missing versionRuntimeCuDNN usage')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] Convolution.cpp uses versionRuntimeCuDNN')

    # Check sdp_utils.cpp uses versionRuntimeCuDNN
    with open('aten/src/ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.cpp', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('sdp_utils.cpp: missing versionRuntimeCuDNN usage')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] sdp_utils.cpp uses versionRuntimeCuDNN')

    # Check cudnn.cpp uses CUDAHooks interface
    with open('torch/csrc/cuda/shared/cudnn.cpp', 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
        if 'getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN()' not in content:
            errors.append('cudnn.cpp: missing getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN() call')
        else:
            print('  [PASS] cudnn.cpp uses getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN()')

    print()
    if errors:
        print('ERRORS:')
        for err in errors:
            print(f'  {err}')
        return 1
    else:
        print('All 7 modified files validated successfully!')
        print()
        print('Modified files:')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/Context.h')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.cpp')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.h')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/native/Convolution.cpp')
        print('  - aten/src/ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.cpp')
        print('  - torch/csrc/cuda/shared/cudnn.cpp')
        print()
        print('cuDNN runtime version is now exposed through CUDA hooks.')
        return 0

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main())

# [cuDNN][SDPA][Convolution] Expose cuDNN runtime version in CUDA hooks

**Repository:** pytorch  
**Difficulty:** HARD  
**Category:** cross_module_bug_fix



## Description

cuDNN dispatching heuristics rely on versions checks but currently only that compile-time version is exposed, if we want to allow users to resolve https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166643 on their end by updating their cuDNN version locally we need to check the runtime version rather than compile-time version.

Test plan: add test comparing performance with cuDNN 9.15+ when disabling and enabling convolutino explicilty for 3D cases.

Also partially covered by smoke test PR: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/165922/files

.PHONY: local
local:

.PHONY: android

.PHONY: ios
ios:
	@./scripts/build_ios.sh

.PHONY: triton

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean
ensure-branch-clean:
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env-rocm

	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."

	@echo "Generating .lintrunner.sha256..."
	@mkdir -p .lintbin

	fi

.PHONY: lint
lint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --all-files

.PHONY: quickfix

	lintrunner --apply-patches

SHELL        = /bin/bash

.PHONY: all

		cmake --build build --parallel --

.PHONY: local
local:

.PHONY: android
android:

.PHONY: ios
ios:
	@./scripts/build_ios.sh

.PHONY: triton
triton:

	@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean

	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)

.PHONY: setup-lint
setup-lint .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256: requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml
	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."
	$(PIP) install lintrunner
	lintrunner init
	@echo "Generating .lintrunner.sha256..."
	@mkdir -p .lintbin
	@sha256sum requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml > .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256

.PHONY: lazy-setup-lint
lazy-setup-lint: .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256
	@if [ ! -x "$(shell command -v lintrunner)" ]; then \
		$(MAKE) setup-lint; \
	fi

.PHONY: lint
lint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --all-files

.PHONY: quicklint
quicklint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner

.PHONY: quickfix

	lintrunner --apply-patches
